summary: On 2026-04-08, Israeli airstrikes hit several commercial and residential areas in central Beirut, Lebanon. The article says the strikes killed over 100 people and caused heavy destruction in the city center. No Hezbollah retaliatory strikes were reported in the first hours after the attack.

title: Air/drone strike
summary: On 2026-03-12, airstrikes hit the headquarters of the Popular Mobilization Forces in Akashat, western Anbar Province, Iraq. Iraqi media reported at least 30 killed and dozens wounded, and said at least three PMF 19th Brigade sites were targeted. The attacker was not identified in the reporting.

title: Air/drone strike
summary: On 2026-03-12, an airstrike reportedly hit positions belonging to Iraq’s Popular Mobilization Forces between Akashat and al-Qaim in Anbar province. The report said around 30 people were killed and dozens wounded. The article did not identify the attacker.

title: Israeli airstrikes kill at least 27 in Lebanon; PM says no end in sight
summary: On 2026-04-02, Israeli forces carried out airstrikes and ground attacks across Lebanon, according to the article. Lebanon’s Health Ministry said 27 people were killed and 105 wounded in the last 24 hours, with a family of four killed in a Nabatieh airstrike. The article also says the Israeli military claimed to have killed Hezbollah fighters during ground operations.

title: Shelling/artillery/missile attack

Iranian state television and local media said at least 26 people were killed and rescue operations were ongoing. The report says the target area was entirely civilian residential land.

More than 25 people were killed, including 12 Lebanese State Security officers; the remaining victims were reported to be civilians. The strikes were described as among the most intense attacks on the town since figh...

title: Vance leads Islamabad talks with Iran
summary: The White House confirmed that Vice President JD Vance would lead the US negotiating team in direct talks with Iran in Islamabad on April 10. The announcement came as the ceasefire framework showed signs of strain with Iran accusing the US of violating three clauses of the agreed framework.

title: White House demands Hormuz reopen
summary: The White House issued a direct demand that Iran immediately reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning that failure to comply would have serious consequences for the ceasefire negotiations. The demand came 24 hours after Iranian state media announced the second closure of the strategic waterway.

title: 182 killed in central Beirut strikes
summary: Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed 182 people were killed in Israeli airstrikes on central Beirut, marking the highest single-day death toll in the Israel-Hezbollah war. The strikes targeted the Basta neighborhood and other central districts. Among the dead were two Lebanese journalists from Al Jazeera and other media organizations covering the conflict.

title: Iran closes Hormuz for second time
summary: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz for a second time following Israeli strikes on Lebanon, threatening the fragile ceasefire framework hours after the US-Iran agreement was announced. Iranian state media reported the closure was a proportional response to Israel's violation of the ceasefire's geographic scope.

title: Hormuz ceasefire opens shipping lane
summary: Trump announced a US-Iran 2-week ceasefire effective immediately. Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz under monitoring. Iran's Supreme National Security Council confirmed acceptance. Talks will begin in Islamabad on Friday. The announcement came minutes before Trump's 8PM ET deadline.

title: IDF largest coordinated Lebanon strikes
summary: The Israeli Air Force conducted its largest coordinated strike campaign against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon since the start of the war. IDF struck Beirut's southern suburbs, the Bekaa Valley, and southern Lebanon simultaneously. Lebanese Ministry of Health reported 14 killed and hundreds wounded in initial reports, with later tallies reaching 300+ casualties. The strikes came hours after Netanyahu confirmed the Iran...

title: Synagogue — Tehran
summary: A synagogue in Tehran was 'completely destroyed' in a US-Israeli strike overnight April 7, prompting international outrage. Reports indicated 15 people were killed across Iran overnight as part of a sustained wave of attacks. This marked the first confirmed strike on a religious/civilian cultural site in Tehran during the conflict.

title: Alba — Aluminium Bahrain industrial complex
summary: Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) confirmed its facilities were targeted in an Iranian attack, with two people mildly injured. The IRGC explicitly stated it targeted both Alba (Bahrain) and Emirates Global Aluminium (UAE) in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on two Iranian steel plants. Damage assessment ongoing. Alba is one of the world's largest aluminium smelters.

title: EGA Al Taweelah Aluminium Complex, KEZAD
location: EGA Al Taweelah Aluminium Complex, KEZAD
summary: Iran struck the UAE's Khalifa Economic Zone (KEZAD) with cruise missiles, BMs, and drones — hitting Emirates Global Aluminium's Al Taweelah complex (significant damage, multiple injuries) and causing 3 fires and 6 civilian injuries. The IRGC explicitly stated both EGA (UAE) and Alba (Bahrain) were targeted as deliberate retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on two Iranian steel plants (Khuzestan + Mobarakeh). Iran's Dub...

metadata.target: EGA Al Taweelah Aluminium Complex, KEZAD

title: KRG President Barzani Residence, Duhok
summary: The residence of Nechirvan Barzani, president of Iraq's semi-autonomous Kurdistan Region, was struck by a drone in Duhok, causing damage but no casualties. The home was empty at the time. No group claimed responsibility. Iraqi PM al-Sudani condemned the 'heinous act'; a joint federal-KRG security investigation launched.

title: PMF position, Kirkuk area
summary: A strike in the Kirkuk area of northern Iraq killed three Popular Mobilization Forces fighters and wounded four others. The PMF (Hashed al-Shaabi) blamed the US and Israel, calling it a 'treacherous Zionist-American attack.' Neither the IDF nor US CENTCOM immediately claimed responsibility.

title: Underwater Research Centre — Isfahan, Iran
summary: The IDF, guided by Israeli navy intelligence, struck the Iranian Underwater Research Centre in Isfahan — described as the only facility in Iran responsible for the design and development of submarines and support systems for the Iranian navy. The IDF confirmed the centre also produced various types of unmanned naval vessels. The strike is a direct retaliation for Iran's 101-missile attack on the USS Abraham Lincoln. ...

title: Dallafa Bridge
summary: Israel's military struck the Dallafa Bridge in southern Lebanon on Monday, the latest in a series of strikes on vital transit routes. Israeli officials said the attacks target Hezbollah logistics and fighter movement from northern Lebanon toward the south. Lebanese civilians have also relied on the bridges to flee fighting in southern Lebanon.

title: Qasmiya Bridge — Litani River (DESTROYED)
summary: Israel destroyed five bridges over the Litani River in southern Lebanon, severing Hezbollah's supply routes and civilian movement south of the river. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun condemned the strikes as 'a dangerous escalation and a blatant violation of Lebanon's sovereignty,' calling them 'a prelude to a ground invasion.' Aoun said the targeting 'constitutes an attempt to sever the geographic connection between t...
